english: yaṅ-kaṅ* (yang kang*) accustomed to man, tame, also without *mi daṅ* (*mi dang*) W.; *yaṅ-kyád* (*yang kyad*) custom, use, habit W. — 4. to accumulate (?) Cs. — 5. to conjure to the spot, to call by magic (?) Tar. 76,15 Schf.

I. vb., pf. and fut. sbyar (sbyar), W. *źar-če* (*zhar ce*), trs. of °byór-ba ('byor ba), 1. to affix, attach, fasten, stick, a writing, a plaster W.: *źar gyab-če* (*zhar rgyab ce*); to apply lcé-rtse dkáṅ-la (lce rtse dkang la) Gram.; fig. bló-la (blo la), séms-la (sems la) to impress; *kár-ya daṅ* (*dkar ya dang*) to solder W.; *źar gyáb-la źor* (*zhar rgyab la zhor*) nail it fast! W.; *me-skám źar tsár* (*me skam zhar tsar*) the trigger is drawn W.; to put on, a plaster, v. above, an arrow on the bow-string; to subjoin, take up, resume, a subject in a treatise Thgy., Tar. 127,14; to put together, to join, unite, rús-pa čág-pa (rus pa cag pa) Med., dbáṅ-po gnyis (dbang po gnyis) v. sub II.; to compile, compose, a book; k̇a (kha) 1. to close, shut, one's mouth, = °táms-pa (tams pa) Pth. 2. to kiss C.; to insert, to dispose in proper classes or divisions Gram., byá-bai sgra ma sbyar yaṅ (bya ba'i sgra ma sbyar yang) also without the word bya (bya) being added; bdé-ba-la (bde ba la), byaṅ-čúb-la (byang cub la) Mil., like °gód-pa (god pa) 3; to join, connect, combine, words, letters; tsíg de don daṅ sbyár-tsa-na (tshig de don dang sbyar tsa na) if these sentences are joined with their significations, i.e. if their explanation is given Mil.; rtsís-su (rtsis su) to count together, to sum up Dzl.; sbyór-la (sbyor la), gen. written źor-la (zhor la), joined, connected, combined, *tsíg nyi sum źór-la yoṅ* (*tshig nyi sum zhor la yong*) two or three words are found joined to one another; this word is frq. used to express simultaneousness of action, where in English expressions as 'along with', 'together with', 'at the same time' etc. are used: źor-la °gró-ba (zhor la 'gro ba) to go along with (another person) Mil.; źór-la kur-kyer (zhor la khur khyer) take this also along with it! *ko čá-te źor daṅ kal soṅ* (*kho ca te zhor dang bkal song*) W. as he was going, we sent it along with him; źor-la gyel soṅ (zhor la rgyel song) it fell at the same time (by coming in contact with some other falling body); *źór-la kyér-wa* (*zhor la khyer wa*) to take hold of and take away at the same time; kó-la źor póg-pa (kho la zhor phog pa) he was also (simultaneously) affected by (the loss); *źór-la zér-kaṅ źig* (*zhor la zer kang zhig*) or even *tsíg-gi źor* (*tshig gi zhor*) a mere expletive, without any appreciable meaning C.; bdag sdig-sgríb čés-pai źór-la (bdag sdig sgrib ces pa'i zhor la) (the calamity has befallen the others too), owing to their connection with such a great sinner as I am Mil. nt. — 2. to prepare, procure, to get ready, yo-byád (yo byad) the appurtenances Dzl., °tsó-ba (tsho ba) victuals Dzl.; rta daṅ sbyár-bai śiṅ-rta (rta dang sbyar ba'i shing rta) a carriage ready to start Stg. (or acc. to no. 1, a carriage attached to the horses); to mix, ču daṅ (chu dang) with water Dzl. and elsewh.; gźán-du (gzhan du) to prepare, to turn one thing into another, to change, transform Thgy.; frq. to prepare one's own mind, to compose one's self, dád-pa-la sbyór-bar gyis (dad pa la sbyor bar gyis) make up your mind to believe Mil. — to join, fit together, adjust, make agree, esp. one's course of action; to conform one's self to, with daṅ (dang), k̇ó-moi yid daṅ sbyor čig (kho mo'i yid dang sbyor cig) accommodate yourself to my wishes Dzl.; krims daṅ sbyár-ro (khrims dang sbyar ro) Dzl. then we must conform to the law; most frq.: ... daṅ sbyár-nas (dang sbyar nas) or -te (-te) corresponding, agreeable to, according to, krims (khrims) according to the law, to usage etc. Dzl.; bú-moi yid (bu mo'i yid) according to the wish of the daughter Dzl.; also to compare Tar. 89, 16, Thgy.; gźan-rgyúd-la sbyór-ba (gzhan rgyud la sbyor ba) seems to imply: to gain knowledge by observing others, opp. to raṅ-rgyúd-la brtág-pa (rang rgyud la brtag pa), to ascertain by one's own immediate judgment. 4. to compose poetry, ... kyis sbyár-bao (kyis sbyar ba'o) = sdeb-pa (sdeb pa) 5 — II. sbst. 1. adjunction, conjunction, union, dbáṅ-po gnyis-kyi sbyór-ba byéd-pa (dbang po gnyis kyi sbyor ba byed pa), 'membrorum amborum conjunctionem efficere' Wdn.; hence coition, cohabitation, bud-méd-la sbyór-ba byéd-pa to (bud med la sbyor ba byed pa to) effectuate it with a woman Pth.; sgra-sbyor-ba (sgra sbyor ba) a joining or combination of sounds (letters), orthography (?) Zam. — 2. a mingling, a mixture, e.g. of medicines, also sbyar-tábs (sbyar thabs) Med.; sbyor-sde-bźi (sbyor sde bzhi) the four departments of pharmacy Glr. (apparently the title of a book); preparation = sṅón-°gro (sngon 'gro) Schl. 240, also mental preparation, esp. the preparation of the mind for prayer, and the arrangement of it, meditation preparatory to it (nif.) cf. mťsams sbyár-ba (mtshams sbyar ba). — 3. syllogism Was. (278). — 4. comparison, agreement, harmony, gtám-gyi (gtam gyi) the harmony of history Schf.
1. W. *(s)bra* (*(s)bra*), C. *da* (*da*) felt-tent, sbra-gúr (sbra gur) id.; sbra-tág (sbra thag) ropes, sbra-śiṅ (sbra shing)
